name: HMS L 26 (N 26)
events: Wreckage of L26 rediscovered during the 1998/1999 investigation of Swiss-air flight 111 crash. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HMS_L26 http://youtu.be/ULvDoh1KMw0 (1)
The history of HMS L 26 as compiled on this page is extracted from the patrol reports and logbooks of this submarine.
This page was last updated in February 2019.
31 Aug 1939 HMS L 26 (Lt.Cdr. F.W. Lipscomb, OBE, RN) departed Blyth for her 1st war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the North Sea.
For the daily positions of HMS L 26 during this patrol see the map below.
13 Sep 1939 HMS L 26 (Lt.Cdr. F.W. Lipscomb, OBE, RN) ended her 1st war patrol at Blyth. (3)
19 Sep 1939 HMS L 26 (Lt.Cdr. F.W. Lipscomb, OBE, RN) is docked at Blyth. (3)
21 Sep 1939 HMS L 26 (Lt.Cdr. F.W. Lipscomb, OBE, RN) is undocked. (3)
30 Sep 1939 HMS L 26 (Lt.Cdr. F.W. Lipscomb, OBE, RN) departed Blyth for her 2nd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the North Sea.
11 Oct 1939 HMS L 26 (Lt.Cdr. F.W. Lipscomb, OBE, RN) ended her 2nd war patrol at Blyth. (4)
14 Oct 1939 HMS L 26 (Lt.Cdr. F.W. Lipscomb, OBE, RN) shifted from Blyth to Rosyth. (5)
28 Oct 1939 HMS L 26 (Lt.Cdr. F.W. Lipscomb, OBE, RN) departed Rosyth for her 3rd war patrol. She is ordered to patrol in the North Sea.
[As there is no log available for this period and the patrol report does not give daily positions, no map can be displayed.] (2)

7 Nov 1939 At 1648 hours, HMS Cachalot (Lt.Cdr. S.W.F. Bennetts, RN), made rendez-vous with HMS Seal (Lt.Cdr. R.P. Lonsdale, RN), HMS L 26 (Lt.Cdr. F.W. Lipscomb, OBE, RN) and their escort towards Portsmouth, HMS Boadicea (Lt.Cdr. G.B. Kingdon, RN) near the North Goodwin light vessel. (6)
8 Nov 1939 HMS L 26 (Lt.Cdr. F.W. Lipscomb, OBE, RN) ended her 3rd war patrol at Portsmouth where she was immediately taken in hand for refit.
From completion of her refit in the second week of January 1940 she participated in submarine training at Portsmouth (training classes).
[As no log is available for this period no further details regarding this training can be provided.] (5)
10 May 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. P.L. Field, RN) departed Portsmouth for her 4th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the North Sea.

18 May 1940 At 0450 hours, in position 52°23'N, 03°05'E, HMS L 26 (Lt. P.L. Field, RN), found an open boat containing one women and eight men. They came from IJmuiden, the Netherlands and were taken on board.
HMS L 26 ended her 4th war patrol at Harwich later the same day. (2)
22 May 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. P.L. Field, RN) departed Harwich for her 5th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the North Sea.
[As there is no log available for this period and the patrol report does not give daily positions, no map can be displayed.] (4)
31 May 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. P.L. Field, RN) ended her 5th war patrol at Harwich. (4)
1 Jun 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. P.L. Field, RN) shifted from Harwich to Sheerness. (4)
2 Jun 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. P.L. Field, RN) is docked at the Sheerness Dockyard for repairs.
[The date of undocking is currently not known to us.] (4)
18 Jun 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. P.L. Field, RN) departed Sheerness for Dundee. She made the passage in a convoy. (5)
20 Jun 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. P.L. Field, RN) arrived at Dundee. (5)
27 Jun 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. P.L. Field, RN) departed Dundee for Scapa Flow. She was escorted by HrMs Z 5 (Lt.Cdr. W. van Lier, RNN) arrive Dundee. (4)
28 Jun 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. P.L. Field, RN) arrived at Scapa Flow to participate in A/S training exercises.
[As there are no logs available for this period, no further details can be given.] (4)
28 Jul 1940 Around 0700 A/28, HrMs O 23 (Lt.Cdr. G.B.M. van Erkel, RNN) and her escort, the the Dutch torpedo boat HrMs Z 6 (Lt.Cdr. A.C. Lunbeck, RNN) departed Stornoway for Rosyth.
Around 1730 A/28, off Scapa Flow they were joined by the British submarine HMS L 26 (Lt. P.L. Field, RN) which had just departed from there also for Rosyth. (7)
29 Jul 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. P.L. Field, RN) arrived at Rosyth. (5)

29 Jul 1940 Around 1100 A/29, HrMs O 23 (Lt.Cdr. G.B.M. van Erkel, RNN), HMS L 26 (Lt. P.L. Field, RN) and their escort, the the Dutch torpedo boat HrMs Z 6 (Lt.Cdr. A.C. Lunbeck, RNN) arrived at Rosyth. (7)
3 Aug 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. J.B.de B. Kershaw, RN) shifted from Rosyth to Dundee. (5)
7 Aug 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. J.B. de B. Kershaw, RN) is docked at Dundee. (4)
8 Aug 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. J.B. de B. Kershaw, RN) is undocked. (4)
11 Aug 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. J.B. de B. Kershaw, RN) departed Dundee for Scapa Flow. She was escorted by HrMs Z 5 (Lt.Cdr. W. van Lier, RNN). (4)
12 Aug 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. J.B. de B. Kershaw, RN) arrived at Scapa Flow to participate in A/S training exercises.
8 Sep 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. J.B. de B. Kershaw, RN) departed Scapa Flow for Dundee. (5)
9 Sep 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. J.B. de B. Kershaw, RN) arrived at Dundee. While entering the lock gates she damaged herself. (8)
14 Sep 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. J.B. de B. Kershaw, RN) is docked at Dundee for repairs to the hydroplanes. (4)
18 Sep 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. J.B. de B. Kershaw, RN) is undocked. (4)
20 Sep 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. J.B. de B. Kershaw, RN) is docked at Dundee. (4)
18 Oct 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. J.B. de B. Kershaw, RN) is undocked. (4)
26 Oct 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. J.B. de B. Kershaw, RN) departed Dundee for Scapa Flow. (4)
27 Oct 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. J.B. de B. Kershaw, RN) arrived at Scapa Flow to participate in A/S training exercises.
10 Dec 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. J.B. de B. Kershaw, RN) departed Scapa Flow for Dundee. (4)
11 Dec 1940 HMS L 26 (Lt. J.B. de B. Kershaw, RN) arrived at Dundee. (4)
26 Dec 1940 HMS L 26 shifted from Dundee to Rosyth where she was to participate in A/S exercises.
[As there are no logs available for this period, no further details can be given.] (5)
13 Feb 1941 HMS L 26 (Lt. R.P. Raikes, RN) shifted from Rosyth to Dundee. En-route she was attacked by a German Ju-88 bomber which dropped four bombs but they fell wide and caused no damage. (9)
17 Mar 1941 HMS L 26 (Lt. S.L.C. Maydon, RN) departed Dundee for Rothesay. She was escorted by HMS White Bear (Cdr.(Retd.) C.C. Flemming, RN). (10)
18 Mar 1941 HMS L 26 (Lt. S.L.C. Maydon, RN) arrived at Rothesay. (10)
20 Mar 1941 HMS L 26 (Lt. S.L.C. Maydon, RN) conducted A/S exercises in the Clyde area with HMS Legion (Cdr. R.F. Jessel, RN). (10)

21 Mar 1941 During 21/22 March 1941, HMS Oberon (Lt.Cdr. E.F. Pizey, DSO, RN), HMS H 44 (Lt. W.N.R. Knox, DSC, RN) and HMS L 26 (Lt. S.L.C. Maydon, RN) all conducted exercises off Campbeltown with each other.
Upon completion of these exercises HMS Oberon and HMS L 26 proceeded to Rothesay, HMS H 44 proceeded to Campbeltown. (11)
22 Mar 1941 HMS L 26 (Lt. S.L.C. Maydon, RN) departed Rothesay for her 6th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Bay of Biscay.

Passage south through the Irish Sea was made together with HMS Torbay (Lt.Cdr. A.C.C. Miers, RN), HMS Tuna (Lt.Cdr. M.K. Cavenagh-Mainwaring, DSO, RN) and HMS Taku (Lt. J.F.B. Brown, RN). They were escorted by the Free French minesweeper FFS La Moqueuse.
13 Apr 1941 HMS L 26 (Lt. S.L.C. Maydon, RN) ended her 6th war patrol at Falmouth.
She proceeded to Plymouth later the same day for repairs at the Devonport Dockyard. (12)
20 May 1941 With her repairs completed, HMS L 26 (Lt. M.B. St. John, RN), departed Plymouth for Rothesay. She was escorted by HrMs Z 5 (Lt. J.J. Steensma, RNN). (13)
22 May 1941 HMS L 26 (Lt. M.B. St. John, RN) arrived at Rothesay. (13)
25 May 1941 HMS L 26 (Lt. M.B. St. John, RN) shifted from Rothesay to Tobermory. She was escorted by HMS Tarantella (T/Lt. the Hon. R.A. Balfour, RNVR). (13)
28 May 1941 HMS L 26 (Lt. M.B. St. John, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Polruan (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) J.S. Landers, RNR) and HMIS Jumna (Cdr. W.R. Shewring, RIN). (13)
29 May 1941 HMS L 26 (Lt. M.B. St. John, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Polruan (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) J.S. Landers, RNR) and HMIS Jumna (Cdr. W.R. Shewring, RIN). (13)
4 Jun 1941 HMS L 26 (Lt. M.B. St. John, RN) participated in A/S exercises off Tobermory. (14)

30 Jun 1941 HMS L 26 (Lt. M.B. St. John, RN) departed Tobermory for Rothesay. (14)
1 Jul 1941 HMS L 26 (Lt. M.B. St. John, RN) arrived at Rothesay.
[No logs are available for the period July 1941 until the start of her refit in September so no more details can be given other then the ones currently listed.]
7 Jul 1941 HMS L 26 (Lt. M.B. St. John, RN) shifted from Rothesay to Ardrossan for repairs. (5)
28 Jul 1941 With her repairs completed, HMS L 26 (Lt. M.B. St. John, RN), shifted from Ardrossan to Rothesay. (5)
2 Aug 1941 HMS L 26 (Lt. M.B. St. John, RN) shifted from Rothesay to Campbeltown for a period of A/S exercises. (15)
27 Aug 1941 HMS L 26 (Lt. M.B. St. John, RN) shifted from Campbeltown to Rothesay. (15)
12 Sep 1941 HMS L 26 (Lt. M.B. St. John, RN) departed Rothesay for Plymouth. She was escorted by HMS Alecto (Cdr. R.M.G. Gambier, RN). (9)
14 Sep 1941 HMS L 26 (Lt. M.B. St. John, RN) arrived at Plymouth where she was to refit at the Devonport Dockyard. (5)
17 Feb 1942 With her refit completed, HMS L 26 (Lt. H.D. Verschoyle, DSC, RN), conducted trials and exercises off Plymouth. (16)
18 Feb 1942 HMS L 26 (Lt. H.D. Verschoyle, DSC, RN) conducted exercises off Plymouth. Upon completion of these she departed for Rothesay escorted by HMS Breda (Capt.(Retd.) A.E. Johnston, RN). (16)
